steps.title: Find the slope of line p by converting to slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Start with the equation of line $p$: $4y + 8x = 6$.
Divide all terms by 4:
$$y + 2x = \frac{3}{2}$$
Subtract $2x$ from both sides to isolate $y$:
$$y = -2x + \frac{3}{2}$$
The slope of line $p$ is $m_p = -2$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular lines shortcut
steps.content:
For perpendicular lines, the product of their slopes is $-1$. Substitute $m_p = -2$:
$$(-2) \cdot m_r = -1$$
$$m_r = \frac{-1}{-2} = \frac{1}{2}$$
The slope of line $r$ is $\frac{1}{2}$ or \$0.5$.
